Building a REST API with Express.js

In this tutorial, we'll build a simple REST API using Express.js.

Step 1: Setup

First, create a new file called server.js:

const express = require('express');
const app = express();
const PORT = process.env.PORT || 3000;

app.use(express.json());

app.get('/', (req, res) => {
  res.json({ message: 'Welcome to the API' });
});

app.listen(PORT, () => {
  console.log(`Server running on port ${PORT}`);
});

Step 2: Add Routes

Create a routes file at routes/users.js:

const express = require('express');
const router = express.Router();

let users = [];

router.get('/', (req, res) => {
  res.json(users);
});

router.post('/', (req, res) => {
  const { name, email } = req.body;
  const user = { id: users.length + 1, name, email };
  users.push(user);
  res.status(201).json(user);
});

module.exports = router;

Step 3: Package.json

{
  "name": "express-api",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "main": "server.js",
  "scripts": {
    "start": "node server.js"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "express": "^4.18.0"
  }
}

Step 4: Dockerfile

FROM node:18-alpine
WORKDIR /app
COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm install
COPY . .
EXPOSE 3000
CMD ["node", "server.js"]
